# nuxt-directus-sdk

> The comprehensive Nuxt module for seamless Directus CMS integration

- GitHub: https://github.com/rolleyio/nuxt-directus-sdk
- Docs: https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io

## Overview

nuxt-directus-sdk is a production-ready Nuxt module that provides complete integration with Directus CMS. It handles authentication, realtime updates, file management, visual editing, type generation, and more — all with minimal configuration.

## Guides

- [Getting Started](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/guide/getting-started) — Installation, setup, Directus config, split URLs for Docker/K8s
- [Authentication](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/guide/authentication) — Session-based auth, SSO/OAuth, user management, protected routes
- [Realtime & WebSockets](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/guide/realtime) — Live subscriptions, auth modes, dev proxy
- [Visual Editor](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/guide/visual-editor) — Iframe detection, inline editing, debug mode
- [Server-Side Utilities](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/guide/server-side) — Server routes, admin operations, token auth

## API Reference

- [Configuration](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/api/configuration) — Module options, env vars, Directus server config
- [Composables](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/api/composables) — useDirectusAuth, useDirectus, file helpers, and more
- [Components](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/api/components) — DirectusVisualEditor, DirectusEditButton, DirectusAddButton

## Installation

```bash
npm install nuxt-directus-sdk
```

```typescript
// nuxt.config.ts
export default defineNuxtConfig({
  modules: ['nuxt-directus-sdk'],
  directus: {},
})
```

```dotenv
# .env
DIRECTUS_URL=https://your-directus.com
DIRECTUS_ADMIN_TOKEN=your-admin-token
```

## Core Features

### Authentication
- Session-based auth with httpOnly cookies
- Auto-login on app load
- SSO/OAuth support (Google, GitHub, Microsoft, etc.)
- User management (register, password reset, invites)
- Protected routes with `auth` and `guest` middleware

```typescript
const { login, logout, user, loggedIn } = useDirectusAuth()
await login('user@email.com', 'password')
await loginWithProvider('google')
```

### Realtime & WebSockets
- Full WebSocket support with auto authentication
- Live collection subscriptions
- Dev proxy handles WebSocket connections at `/directus-ws`

```typescript
await directus.connect()
const { subscription } = await directus.subscribe('posts')
for await (const message of subscription) {
  console.log('Update:', message)
}
```

### File Management
- Single and batch file uploads
- Image transformations (resize, format, quality)
- Smart URL generation

```typescript
await uploadDirectusFile({ file: myFile })
const url = getDirectusFileUrl(file, { width: 300, format: 'webp' })
```

### Visual Editor
- Automatic iframe detection — no query parameters needed
- Inline editing with drawer, modal, or popover modes
- DirectusVisualEditor, DirectusEditButton, and DirectusAddButton components
- Automatic data refresh on save (no full page reload)
- Debug mode via `?debug` for troubleshooting
- MutationObserver-based detection for reliable SSR hydration

```vue
<DirectusVisualEditor collection="posts" :item="post.id">
  <h1>{{ post.title }}</h1>
</DirectusVisualEditor>

<DirectusEditButton collection="posts" :item="post.id" />
<DirectusAddButton collection="posts" :item="post.id" field="blocks" />
```

### TypeScript & Development
- Auto-generated types from Directus schema
- Auto-imports for 80+ Directus SDK functions
- Dev proxy eliminates CORS issues
- Directus admin in Nuxt DevTools
- Split URL support for Docker/K8s (`{ client, server }`)

## Key Composables

### useDirectusAuth()
```typescript
const {
  user,                 // Ref<DirectusUsers | null>
  loggedIn,            // ComputedRef<boolean>
  login,               // (email, password, options?) => Promise
  loginWithProvider,   // (provider, redirect?) => Promise
  logout,              // (redirect?) => Promise
  register,            // (data) => Promise (alias: createUser)
  readMe,              // () => Promise
  updateMe,            // (data) => Promise
  inviteUser,          // (email, role, inviteUrl?) => Promise
  acceptUserInvite,    // (token, password) => Promise
  passwordRequest,     // (email, resetUrl?) => Promise
  passwordReset,       // (token, password) => Promise
} = useDirectusAuth()
```

### useDirectus()
Returns the full Directus SDK client with `request()`, `connect()`, `subscribe()`, and all SDK methods.

### useDirectusUrl(path?)
Context-aware URL generation (uses proxy in dev, direct in prod, prefers server URL during SSR).

### useDirectusOriginUrl(path?)
Always returns the public-facing Directus URL (ignores devProxy and server URL). Use for SSO redirects and admin links.

### useDirectusVisualEditor()
Returns `Ref<boolean>` — true when site is inside a Directus iframe. Set automatically.

### useDirectusPreview()
Returns `Ref<boolean>` — activated via `?preview=true`. Separate from visual editor mode.

### Server-Side Utilities
```typescript
const directus = useServerDirectus(event)     // User's session
const admin = useAdminDirectus()              // Admin token
const custom = useTokenDirectus(token)        // Custom token
const token = getDirectusSessionToken(event)  // Manual extraction
```

## Configuration

```typescript
export default defineNuxtConfig({
  directus: {
    // Core — simple string or split URLs for Docker/K8s
    url: process.env.DIRECTUS_URL,
    // url: { client: 'https://cms.example.com', server: 'http://directus:8055' },
    adminToken: process.env.DIRECTUS_ADMIN_TOKEN,

    auth: {
      enabled: true,
      autoRefresh: true,
      credentials: 'include',
      realtimeAuthMode: 'handshake',
      enableGlobalAuthMiddleware: false,
      readMeFields: ['id', 'email', 'first_name', 'last_name', 'avatar', 'role'],
      redirect: { login: '/auth/login', home: '/', logout: '/' },
    },

    devProxy: { enabled: true, path: '/directus', wsPath: '/directus-ws' },
    types: { enabled: true, prefix: '' },
    image: true,         // @nuxt/image Directus provider
    devtools: true,
    visualEditor: true,
  },
})
```

## Components

### DirectusVisualEditor
Wrapper that marks content as editable via `data-directus` attributes (only inside Directus iframe).
Props: `collection` (required), `item` (required), `fields` (optional: string | string[]), `mode` (optional: 'drawer' | 'modal' | 'popover')

### DirectusEditButton
Floating fixed-position button (bottom-right) to open Directus editor. Only visible inside iframe.
Props: `collection`, `item`, `mode`

### DirectusAddButton
Full-width dashed inline button for adding items to repeater/relationship fields. Only visible inside iframe.
Props: `collection`, `item`, `field`

## Common Patterns

### Protected Page
```vue
<script setup>
definePageMeta({ middleware: 'auth' })
</script>
```

### Fetch Data
```typescript
const directus = useDirectus()
const { data } = await useAsyncData('posts', () =>
  directus.request(readItems('posts', {
    fields: ['*'],
    filter: { status: { _eq: 'published' } }
  }))
)
```

### Server Route
```typescript
// server/api/data.ts
export default defineEventHandler(async (event) => {
  const directus = useServerDirectus(event)
  return directus.request(readItems('posts'))
})
```

## Auto-Imported SDK Functions

All Directus SDK functions are auto-imported:
- Items: readItems, readItem, createItem, createItems, updateItem, updateItems, deleteItem, deleteItems
- Singletons: readSingleton, updateSingleton
- Files: readFile, readFiles, updateFile, updateFiles, deleteFile, deleteFiles, uploadFiles
- Users: readUser, readUsers, createUser, createUsers, updateUser, updateUsers, deleteUser, deleteUsers, readMe, updateMe
- Collections: readCollection, readCollections, createCollection, updateCollection, deleteCollection
- Fields: readField, readFields, readFieldsByCollection, createField, updateField, deleteField
- Folders: readFolder, readFolders, updateFolder, updateFolders
- Comments: createComment, updateComment, deleteComment
- Activities: readActivity, readActivities
- Auth: readProviders
- Utilities: aggregate, generateUid, withToken, importFile
